title: Entangled-Photon Microscopy (N00N / SPDC imaging)
sub_domain: Phase sensitivity beyond shot-noise via N-photon entanglement
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence template (fan-out artifact). Physics below is grounded in canonical literature for this principle.

E.description: Entangled-Photon Microscopy (N00N / SPDC imaging): entangled photon imaging produces the measurement through a 4-node primitive DAG L.spdc_source -> L.n_photon_interference -> D.coincidence_count -> int.angular, with spatially-projected accumulation and photon-shot-noise-limited (Poisson counting). Recovery is posed as a non-convex inverse problem that inverts the forward operator to estimate the scene-side 2D phase. Difficulty tier delta=10 with effective condition number kappa_eff~30; calibration-level mismatch (photon_loss, mode_coupling_efficiency, detector_dark_counts) sets the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form imaging equation.
E.forward_model: I_N00N(phi) ~ 1 + cos(N * phi); sqrt(N) advantage in phase precision but photon-loss sensitive

W.regime: Existence of the recovered 2D phase is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness is local rather than global (non-convex landscape); convergence depends on initialisation and priors. Stability is moderately conditioned (kappa_eff ~= 30); photon_loss dominates the stability cliff; mode_coupling_efficiency and the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Photon-shot-noise-limited (poisson counting) sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor, while TV / wavelet-sparsity / deep priors stabilise recovery at the ill-conditioned end of Omega.

C.solver_class: linear-operator + convex optimisation [Two-Photon-Interference, HOM-Microscopy] | linear-operator + deep neural prior [Entangled-Net]
C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: ||x_hat - x||_2 <= C1 * sqrt(sigma_signal^2) / sqrt(photon_count). Mismatch-induced bias adds + C2 * |photon_loss| + C3 * ||x - x_s||_1 (best s-sparse / low-rank approximation residual). Iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the MLE; epsilon_fn captures the monotone quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(H * W * log(...)) per iteration; learned variants: O(H W Z * F_theta_cost) per forward pass
